columns.description: America/New_York calendar date derived from the immutable Bronze manifest completed_at timestamp, never from the latest-only request date label.
columns.name: capture_date

columns.description: Massive provider-internal exchange identifier copied from id; it is not the cross-endpoint listing-venue join key.
columns.name: exchange_id

columns.description: Provider exchange or market-venue name preserved verbatim.
columns.name: name

columns.description: Optional provider acronym preserved verbatim.
columns.name: acronym

columns.description: Optional ISO 10383 Market Identifier Code preserved verbatim and used for later assets.primary_exchange joins.
columns.name: mic

columns.description: Optional MIC of the entity operating the venue, preserved verbatim and not substituted for a missing mic.
columns.name: operating_mic

columns.description: Optional SIP participant identifier preserved verbatim without an inferred length or domain.
columns.name: participant_id

columns.description: Provider type copied from type and preserved verbatim, including observed values not listed by the provider documentation.
columns.name: exchange_type

columns.description: Provider asset class; the reviewed U.S. stock request is expected to contain stocks.
columns.name: asset_class

columns.description: Provider locale; the reviewed U.S. stock request is expected to contain us.
columns.name: locale

columns.description: Optional provider website URL preserved verbatim.
columns.name: url

columns.description: Fixed current_reference_snapshot marker that prohibits interpretation as historical venue membership.
columns.name: snapshot_scope

columns.description: Immutable Bronze manifest completed_at timestamp used as the conservative source observation time.
columns.name: source_capture_at_utc

columns.description: First XNYS session whose open is strictly after source_capture_at_utc.
columns.name: available_session

columns.description: XNYS open timestamp for available_session; the snapshot cannot be used before this instant.
columns.name: available_at_utc

columns.description: Fixed first_xnys_open_after_source_capture_v1 point-in-time availability rule.
columns.name: availability_rule

columns.description: Deterministic SHA-256 over dataset, source request, artifact SHA-256, page sequence, row ordinal, and source row hash.
columns.name: source_record_id

columns.description: Canonical local Bronze request ID from the authoritative manifest.
columns.name: source_request_id

columns.description: Server-assigned request ID from the Massive response envelope.
columns.name: source_provider_request_id

columns.description: SHA-256 of the stored gzip Bronze page containing the source row.
columns.name: source_artifact_sha256

columns.description: Zero-based sequence of the source page in the Bronze manifest.
columns.name: source_page_sequence

columns.description: Zero-based ordinal of the source result within its page.
columns.name: source_row_ordinal

columns.description: SHA-256 of the canonical JSON representation of the unmodified provider result object.
columns.name: source_row_hash

description: Current-only Massive U.S. stock exchange reference snapshots with conservative point-in-time availability and row-level Bronze lineage; never a historical membership table.
grain: One provider exchange record per America/New_York capture date, with at most one accepted source snapshot per capture date.

qa_rules.description: Every output Parquet field, order, Arrow type, and nullability must equal this contract.
No authoritative manifest or manifest-bound artifact may fail status, bytes, checksum, or declared-row validation.
Every source page must have an OK envelope, an array of results, a provider request ID, and, when present, a count reconciled to artifact rows.
Each capture date must be backed by exactly one authoritative current-only source request in a build.
Input rows must reconcile exactly to accepted rows, exact duplicate excess, and quarantined source rows.
No row may have a missing, null, blank, or invalid id, name, type, asset_class, or locale.
No capture date and exchange ID may map to different canonical source rows.
The output must contain no duplicate excess for the frozen primary key.
Every output row must reproduce its request, page, ordinal, artifact, row, and record digests.
Capture and availability fields must follow the frozen calendar version and first-open-after-capture rule.
Every output row must carry the exact current_reference_snapshot scope marker.
Within one capture, a non-null MIC must not identify more than one exchange ID.
Every non-null mic and operating_mic must match four uppercase ASCII letters or digits without normalization.
Every reviewed row must retain the stocks asset class requested from the provider.
Every reviewed row must retain the us locale requested from the provider.
Rows outside the reviewed exchange, ORF, SIP, and TRF source type domain are retained but require an explicit review warning.
Canonical duplicate source rows are deterministically deduplicated and must remain visible in the row funnel.
Rows containing source fields outside the reviewed ten-field mapping are retained for lineage and surfaced for schema review.
Optional source strings that are empty or whitespace-only are preserved and surfaced rather than silently trimmed to null.
Every non-null URL should be an absolute HTTP or HTTPS URL and is otherwise retained with a warning.
